signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + CESI
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.88 testing the 160.00 psychological barrier where BoJ intervention risk historically escalates. Despite JPY net short -86,249 (COT) and a positive JPY CESI (+11), the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) continues to dominate spot direction. Options market remains cautious with a -1.10 25d RR put skew, signaling institutional hedging against a sharp JPY reversal or BoJ action. Carry remains the primary driver until VIX > 25.

signals.title: GBP/CHF — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/CHF at 1.0611 reflects a significant G8 bilateral divergence as GBP outperforms (+0.430%) while CHF laggards (-0.446%). The +375bp carry premium (BoE 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%) favors GBP, further supported by a restrictive GBP real rate of +0.77% vs CHF's accommodative -0.31%. The 0.876% G8 spread confirms a structural flow out of CHF into high-carry G8 peers. Watch for SNB meeting on 18 Jun for potential cut confirmation.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + PMI Shock
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7175 faces immediate pressure after Australia Services PMI slipped to 48.7, signaling contraction. This macro headwind collides with a crowded AUD COT LF net long of +58,041 (76.1% long), creating liquidation risk. Options market is pricing extreme uncertainty with an AUD/USD VRP of +7.4% (IV 15.4% vs HV30 8.0%), the highest in the G10 space. Retail 77% short bias provides a contrarian cushion, but PMI weakness threatens the 0.7150 support level.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Vol Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1631 remains range-bound despite a +3.2% Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.1% vs HV30 3.9%), indicating the options market is paying for protection that realized volatility does not yet justify. While USD CESI (+22) significantly outperforms EUR CESI (+7), the +175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) keeps upside capped. DXY vs SPX correlation at -0.581 (z=+1.68σ) suggests USD is beginning to trade on funding stress rather than pure risk-off flows. Watch ECB meeting on 11 Jun for policy shift.
